originContent: App to sync workouts between TrainingPeaks, TrainerRoad and Intervals.icu.
translatedContent: App to sync workouts between TrainingPeaks, TrainerRoad and Intervals.icu.

originContent: Runs on MacOS (DMG), Windows (EXE installer), Linux (AppImage). Alternatively there is Docker image and executable JAR.
translatedContent: Runs on MacOS (DMG), Windows (EXE installer), Linux (AppImage). Alternatively there is Docker image and executable JAR.

originContent: All files are available for download on [Release page](https://github.com/freekode/tp2intervals/releases/latest).
translatedContent: All files are available for download on [Release page](https://github.com/freekode/tp2intervals/releases/latest).

originContent: I don't have access to TrainerRoad anymore. Account, which I used, cancelled subscription. I don't use the platform and it's too expensive to have it for occasional fixes.
translatedContent: I don't have access to TrainerRoad anymore. The account I used canceled the subscription. I don't use the platform and it's too expensive to have it for occasional fixes.

originContent: To fix issues I can only relay on logs and HAR files from you.
translatedContent: To fix issues I can only rely on logs and HAR files from you.

originContent: * Sync planned workouts in calendar between Intervals.icu and TrainingPeaks (for today and tomorrow with free TP account)
translatedContent: * Sync planned workouts in calendar between Intervals.icu and TrainingPeaks (for today and tomorrow with free TP account)

originContent: * Copy whole training plan from TrainingPeaks
translatedContent: * Copy whole training plan from TrainingPeaks

translatedContent: * Create training plan or workout folder on Intervals.icu from planned workouts on TrainingPeaks

originContent: * Copy whole training plan and workout library from TrainingPeaks
translatedContent: * Copy whole training plan and workout library from TrainingPeaks

originContent: * Sync planned workouts in calendar from TrainerRoad to TrainingPeaks or Intervals.icu
translatedContent: * Sync planned workouts in calendar from TrainerRoad to TrainingPeaks or Intervals.icu

originContent: * Copy workouts from TrainerRoad library to Intervals
translatedContent: * Copy workouts from TrainerRoad library to Intervals

translatedContent: * Create training plan or workout folder on Intervals.icu from planned workouts on TrainerRoad

originContent: Automatically schedule workouts for today, by checking your calendar every 20 minutes.
translatedContent: Automatically schedule workouts for today, by checking your calendar every 20 minutes.

originContent: To clear up scheduled jobs just restart the application.
translatedContent: To clear up scheduled jobs just restart the application.
Before using the application you need to configure access to platforms.

originContent: Before using the application you need to configure access to platforms.
translatedContent: Access to Intervals.icu is required, access to other platforms is optional.

originContent: Access to Intervals.icu is required, access to other platforms is optional.

translatedContent: After you gathered all required configuration, you can click Confirm button.

originContent: After you gathered all required configuration, you can click Confirm button.
translatedContent: If everything is fine, you will be redirected to the home page.

originContent: If everything is fine, you will be redirected to the home page.

translatedContent: If your configuration is wrong. You will see an error that there is no access to particular platform.

originContent: If your configuration is wrong. You will see an error that there is no access to particular platform.
translatedContent: Check all your values and save configuration again.

originContent: Check all your values and save configuration again.

translatedContent: Copy API key and Athlete Id from [Settings page](https://intervals.icu/settings) in Developer Settings section on Intervals.icu web page.

originContent: Copy API key and Athlete Id from [Settings page](https://intervals.icu/settings) in Developer Settings section on Intervals.icu web page.
To use TrainingPeaks copy all cookies from request `https://tpapi.trainingpeaks.com/users/v3/token` and put it on Configuration page.
translatedContent: To use TrainingPeaks copy all cookies from request `https://tpapi.trainingpeaks.com/users/v3/token` and put it on Configuration page.

originContent: The app automatically will remove redundant parts and only require cookie will remain. Follow guide below how to do that.
translatedContent: The app automatically will remove redundant parts and only require cookie will remain. Follow guide below how to do that.

originContent: The app requires `Production_tpAuth` cookie (key and value, smth like `Production_tpAuth=very_long_string`).
translatedContent: The app requires `Production_tpAuth` cookie (key and value, smth like `Production_tpAuth=very_long_string`).

originContent: Configuration is very similar to TrainingPeaks. Copy all cookies from request `https://tpapi.trainingpeaks.com/users/v3/token` and put it on Configuration page.
translatedContent: Configuration is very similar to TrainingPeaks. Copy all cookies from request `https://tpapi.trainingpeaks.com/users/v3/token` and put it on Configuration page.

originContent: The app automatically will remove redundant parts and only require cookie will remain. Follow guide below how to do that.
translatedContent: The app automatically will remove redundant parts and only require cookie will remain. Follow guide below how to do that.

originContent: Cookie `SharedTrainerRoadAuth` (key and value, smth like `SharedTrainerRoadAuth=very_long_string`) is required for the app.
translatedContent: Cookie `SharedTrainerRoadAuth` (key and value, smth like `SharedTrainerRoadAuth=very_long_string`) is required for the app.

originContent: Be aware, Firefox cuts long strings in Dev Tool window. Copy cookie value with right click -> Copy Value.
translatedContent: Be aware, Firefox cuts long strings in Dev Tool window. Copy cookie value with right click -> Copy Value.

originContent: * Ramp steps in TrainerRoad are not supported
translatedContent: * Ramp steps in TrainerRoad are not supported

originContent: * **MacOS arm64** Error: `"tp2intervals" is damaged and can’t be opened.`
translatedContent: * **MacOS arm64** Error: `"tp2intervals" is damaged and can’t be opened.`

originContent: Run command in terminal `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/tp2intervals.app` and then open app again
translatedContent: Run command in terminal `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/tp2intervals.app` and then open app again

originContent: * **MacOS** app is not signed. Usually you need to open it twice
translatedContent: * **MacOS** app is not signed. Usually you need to open it twice

originContent: * **Windows** The app will ask to access local network and Internet, you need to allow it. After all it makes HTTP requests
translatedContent: * **Windows** The app will ask to access local network and Internet, you need to allow it. After all it makes HTTP requests

originContent: * More info you can find on the forum https://forum.intervals.icu/t/tp2intervals-copy-trainingpeaks-and-trainerroad-workouts-plans-to-intervals/63375
translatedContent: * More info you can find on the forum https://forum.intervals.icu/t/tp2intervals-copy-trainingpeaks-and-trainerroad-workouts-plans-to-intervals/63375

originContent: ### Info regarding scheduling for the next day with TrainingPeaks free account
translatedContent: ### Info regarding scheduling for the next day with TrainingPeaks free account

originContent: Officially if you have a free TP account, you can't plan workouts for future dates, but practically you can.
translatedContent: Officially if you have a free TP account, you can't plan workouts for future dates, but practically you can.

originContent: You can plan a workout for the next day relative to TrainingPeaks server local time. The server is in UTC-6 time zone. Let's check some examples:
translatedContent: You can plan a workout for the next day relative to TrainingPeaks server local time. The server is in UTC-6 time zone. Let's check some examples:

originContent: Example 1. Your TZ is UTC+2 and current local date time 20.05.2024 06:00. It means at this moment TP server local date time is 19.05.2024 22:00.
translatedContent: Example 1. Your TZ is UTC+2 and current local date time 20.05.2024 06:00. It means at this moment TP server local date time is 19.05.2024 22:00.

originContent: Therefore, you can plan workouts for 20.05.2024. But you can't plan workouts for 21.05.2024, you can do it in 2 hours, because in 2 hours server local time will be 20.05.2024 00:00.
translatedContent: Therefore, you can plan workouts for 20.05.2024. But you can't plan workouts for 21.05.2024, you can do it in 2 hours, because in 2 hours server local time will be 20.05.2024 00:00.

originContent: Example 2. Your TZ is UTC+12, current local date time 20.05.2024 18:00. TP server local date time is 20.05.2024 00:00. At this moment, you can plan workouts for 21.05.2024.
translatedContent: Example 2. Your TZ is UTC+12, current local date time 20.05.2024 18:00. TP server local date time is 20.05.2024 00:00. At this moment, you can plan workouts for 21.05.2024.

originContent: To identify the problems with any platform, logs from the users helps very much.
translatedContent: To identify the problems with any platform, logs from the users helps very much.

originContent: Gather logs from [guide below](#how-to-get-logs). And in case of TrainerRoad platform try to [record HAR file](#how-to-record-har-file). Send the files directly to me.
translatedContent: Gather logs from [guide below](#how-to-get-logs). And in case of TrainerRoad platform try to [record HAR file](#how-to-record-har-file). Send the files directly to me.

originContent: 2. In General section check Debug Mode, click Confirm
translatedContent: 2. In General section check Debug Mode, click Confirm

originContent: 4. Find log file according to your system
translatedContent: 4. Find log file according to your system

originContent: 1. Open new tab in your browser
translatedContent: 1. Open new tab in your browser

originContent: 2. Open dev tools, check Preserve log (Firefox Cog -> Persist Logs)
translatedContent: 2. Open dev tools, check Preserve log (Firefox Cog -> Persist Logs)

originContent: 3. Next steps are very important, as they simulate what the app does.
translatedContent: 3. Next steps are very important, as they simulate what the app does.

originContent: Open TrainerRoad page, open workout library, find any workout, open workout page (the page where you have chart with workout steps, description, alternatives, etc.)
translatedContent: Open TrainerRoad page, open workout library, find any workout, open workout page (the page where you have chart with workout steps, description, alternatives, etc.)

originContent: 4. In dev tools, click Export HAR (Firefox - Cog -> Save All as HAR), save the file and send it to me
translatedContent: 4. In dev tools, click Export HAR (Firefox - Cog -> Save All as HAR), save the file and send it to me
